title: DU professor's murder: Recces, WhatsApp calls and forged IDs point to a plot mapped in advance
content: A Delhi University professor's murder on June 3 appears to have been premeditated by her tenants, who were reportedly angered by her refusal to sell her ancestral home. The couple allegedly conducted reconnaissance and planned the murder after the professor agreed to meet them on June 3.

title: 'Could've been murdered by contract killers if ...': Ex-TMC MP's big claim after quitting party
content: Days after leaving the Trinamool Congress, Sukhendu Sekhar Ray has leveled severe accusations against Mamata Banerjee's party, labeling it a "party of thieves, rapists." He revealed a chilling fear of being "murdered by contract killers" had he departed during the RG Kar hospital controversy, an incident that solidified his decision to leave.

title: Did Trump ask Netanyahu not strike Iran? US President clarifies his position
content: US President Donald Trump stated he urged Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to end hostilities with Iran quickly, not to refrain from retaliation. Trump expressed confidence a nuclear deal with Tehran could be reached within days, suggesting both nations agreed to halt recent exchanges of attacks.

title: Watch: Arjun Tendulkar shines with bat and ball, gets big dressing-room applause
content: Arjun Tendulkar starred for Arcs Andheri in the T20 Mumbai League 2026, delivering a match-winning all-round performance. He took 3/11 with the ball and then smashed an unbeaten 66 to guide his team to a nine-wicket victory. Captain Shivam Dube lauded Tendulkar's effort, highlighting his crucial role in the win.

title: Zojila tunnel breakthrough: Road Pakistan wanted to choke during Kargil gets all-weather shield
content: A major milestone has been achieved in the Zojila Tunnel project, connecting its two ends to create a 13-km passage. This vital infrastructure will ensure all-weather connectivity between Kashmir and Ladakh, eliminating annual disruptions and significantly reducing travel time. Expected to be operational by 2028, the tunnel is crucial for military logistics and regional development.

title: Apple tells everyone 'loud and clear': We are deeply disappointed by Europe
content: Apple's advanced Siri AI upgrade faces an indefinite delay in the EU for iPhones and iPads due to regulatory clashes over privacy and security.  European regulators' interpretation of the Digital Markets Act has prevented Apple from safely integrating the new AI, leaving EU users missing out on key features.  Apple is engaging with regulators for a path forward.

title: TMC vs TMC intensifies: Mamata camp dares 'traitors' to resign and fight on BJP ticket
content: Trinamool MP Kalyan Banerjee fiercely criticized rebel leader Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, labeling her faction "desperate" and accusing them of betraying Mamata Banerjee and colluding with the BJP. Dastidar, however, remained defiant, vowing to fight for Bengal and the nation, asserting her long-standing commitment to the party and its principles. This exchange highlights a deepening internal conflict within the TMC.

title: ED summons Pinarayi Vijayan's daughter Veena in money laundering case
content: T. Veena, daughter of former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan, has been summoned by the Enforcement Directorate for questioning in a money laundering case. The probe centers on alleged financial transactions between Cochin Minerals and Rutile Ltd (CMRL) and her company, Exalogic Solutions. ED claims CMRL made payments of Rs 2.78 crore for IT consultancy, generating proceeds of crime.

title: Google AI CEO Demis Hassabis on AGI: Humans only have a few years left to prepare
content: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis warns humanity is on the cusp of Artificial General Intelligence, potentially within four years, urging immediate preparation. He views current AI agents as a societal stress test and highlights the risks of recursive self-improvement. Hassabis also criticized tech companies for laying off engineers, advocating for increased productivity instead.

title: Karnataka's 5.2 floor area ratio limit: Check how it could impact homebuyers
content: Karnataka has significantly boosted its Floor Area Ratio (FAR) for industrial zones, allowing developers to build taller structures. This move, particularly impactful for Bengaluru's booming real estate market, aims to increase housing supply and potentially ease soaring property prices. However, developers will incur premium fees, which might influence buyer costs.

title: SpiceJet's cash crunch deepens: Many pilots unpaid since March, airline seeks emergency loan
content: SpiceJet pilots and employees are still awaiting salaries owed since March, as the airline seeks an emergency government-backed loan. Internal messages reveal significant financial strain, with employees struggling to manage daily expenses. The airline acknowledges payment delays, attributing them to operational challenges and the Middle East crisis.

title: Ben Stokes controversy: What happened at the Chelsea nightclub?
content: Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson are under ECB investigation after breaching a midnight curfew and becoming involved in a nightclub altercation in Chelsea. Reports claim a Saracens academy rugby player threw a punch at Atkinson but hit an ECB security guard, who needed stitches. ECB sources insist the cricketers “were not the aggressors.” Stokes’s captaincy is now under scrutiny.

title: As Nasa gets ready for Moon, Lauren Sanchez's 'designer uniform' is back
content: NASA's Artemis III mission is advancing with fashion designers contributing to spacesuit development. Axiom Space and Prada unveiled a new inner garment for lunar missions. This follows designer spacesuits worn by Lauren Sanchez on a Blue Origin flight. These collaborations focus on functionality, comfort, and fit for astronauts, including women. The trend highlights fashion's growing role in space exploration.

title: Rs 1.2 lakh penalty on postal department for losing customer’s Pashmina shawls parcel
content: The Baramulla Consumer Commission has delivered a decision, holding postal services accountable for the mishap involving a lost parcel that contained three luxurious Pashmina shawls, collectively valued at Rs 60,000. Despite the postal authorities' reliance on the Indian Post Office Act, the Commission ruled against them, identifying a concrete deficiency in the service provided.

title: What is circle rate and how does it affect how much you pay for a new home?
content: Understanding a property's 'circle rate' is crucial for buyers in India. This government-set minimum value, also known as reckoner or guidance value, dictates stamp duty and registration charges, often exceeding the actual sale price. Location, property type, and infrastructure significantly influence these rates, impacting your final cost and negotiation power. Always verify the circle rate before purchasing.

title: More measures on gold coming? Finance ministry asks banks for information on gold loans
content: Despite a lower import volume of 721 tonnes compared with the previous year, India's gold import bill rose 24% to a record $71.9 billion in 2025-26.

title: How many runs did Sooryavanshi score vs SL A after stellar IPL season?
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s first outing for India A after his impressive IPL 2026 season ended with a 14-run knock off 12 balls against Sri Lanka A in Dambulla. The teenager hit three fours before falling to Mohamed Shiraz. Ahead of the match, Sri Lanka A vice-captain Niroshan Dickwella had praised Sooryavanshi’s talent and said his team had plans to keep him in check.

title: 'Batwara 1947' motion poster: Sunny Deol drops first glimpse; announces release date officially
content: Get ready for an emotional ride as 'Batwara 1947' featuring Sunny Deol and directed by Rajkumar Santoshi reveals its first motion poster! Set against the heart-wrenching backdrop of India’s partition, the film stars a stellar cast including Preity Zinta, Shabana Azmi, and Karan Deol. Originally titled 'Lahore 1947', audiences can anticipate its theatrical debut on August 14, 2026. Read on to know more in detail.

title: More than Butter Chicken: Why India can't stop craving Punjabi food
content: Punjabi cuisine, a cornerstone of Indian comfort food, has endured global culinary shifts by embodying emotion, hospitality, and home. From ancient roots to post-Partition dhabas and modern restaurants, its rich flavors and hearty simplicity continue to resonate, offering a taste of tradition and belonging that nourishes the soul.

title: 'Gukesh can take inspiration from Pragg': Anand advises world champion
content: Viswanathan Anand praised R Praggnanandhaa’s Norway Chess triumph, calling his comeback and fighting style impressive. He urged world champion D Gukesh, who struggled in the event, to take inspiration from Praggnanandhaa’s persistence and hard work. Anand also highlighted the healthy rivalry among India’s top young stars and said India is now firmly among the world’s leading chess nations.

title: 96 hours in no-man's land: BSF shelters Bangladeshi family pushed back by BGB
content: India intervened to shelter a Bangladeshi family of ten stranded near the Jalpaiguri border after Bangladesh refused their return, allegedly seizing their documents. Initially allowed into Bangladesh, they were pushed back within a day. Videos suggest BGB personnel instigated villagers to seize documents, then claimed the family was Indian.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reminds Glenn McGrath of Brian Lara and Garry Sobers
content: Glenn McGrath lauded teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, comparing his fearless style and backlift to legends Brian Lara and Garry Sobers. McGrath was amazed by Sooryavanshi's IPL power-hitting, noting his high six-to-four ratio. He expressed interest in seeing how the young batsman performs outside the subcontinent.

title: Jahangir Khan held after two weeks on the run: How Bengal police tracked down TMC's 'Pushpa'
content: Trinamool candidate Jahangir Khan, nicknamed 'Pushpa', was apprehended near the India-Nepal border after evading arrest for two weeks. Facing multiple criminal charges including murder and extortion, Khan had planned to flee to Nepal. His arrest followed a court vacating his interim protection from arrest.

title: 'This place is HOME!': Virat Kohli's latest 'extra special' RCB post goes viral
content: Virat Kohli celebrated RCB’s back-to-back IPL titles with an emotional post calling the franchise “HOME,” a message that quickly went viral. The star batter played a key role in the 2026 final, scoring an unbeaten 75 as RCB beat Gujarat Titans. Kohli praised the team’s belief and maturity, while also highlighting how younger players continue to push him to improve.

title: US army Apache gunship goes down near Strait of Hormuz: Report
content: A US military Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday amidst escalating tensions with Iran. The incident occurred following a significant military campaign launched by Donald Trump's administration against the Iranian regime. The exact cause of the crash, whether enemy action or mechanical failure, remains unconfirmed.

title: UPSC success story: Born without a forearm, Kajal Raju's journey from AIR 910 to AIR 167
content: Kajal Raju, an IIT Madras graduate born with phocomelia syndrome, achieved her dream of becoming an IAS officer on her fourth attempt, securing AIR 167. Despite initial success with AIR 910 in IRMS, she persevered through setbacks and professional commitments, demonstrating remarkable resilience and dedication to reach her ultimate goal.

title: Rupee rebounds 20 paise to 95.41 against US dollar
content: The Indian rupee strengthened by 20 paise against the US dollar in early trade on Tuesday, opening at 95.47. This gain was attributed to easing oil prices, a weaker US dollar index, and a de-escalation of tensions between Israel and Iran. Domestic equity markets also traded higher, reflecting positive sentiment.

title: Gold falls to nearly 11-week low before recovering; check outlook for today
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices continue to be under pressure as crude oil prices remain high, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: Trump becomes first sitting President to attend NBA Finals game; gets booed by crowd
content: President Donald Trump made history as the first sitting US president to attend an NBA Finals game, but his appearance at Madison Square Garden was met with loud boos. The unprecedented event was overshadowed by extensive security measures and a mixed public reception, highlighting Trump's complex relationship with New York City.

title: Trump and Netanyahu at odds over Iran war? Israeli envoy says 'sometimes lovers have a spat'
content: Despite reports of intensified tensions between President Trump and Prime Minister Netanyahu, Israel's ambassador downplayed a rift, likening it to a "lover's spat." Trump reportedly warned Netanyahu against expanding military operations against Iran, urging restraint to avoid isolation. While the US and Israel collaborated on earlier strikes, differences emerged over diplomatic versus military approaches to the conflict.

title: India's nuclear weapons spending rose in 2025: How does it compare with China & Pak ?
content: Global spending on nuclear weapons hit an all-time high of nearly $119 billion in 2025, a 19% increase, fueling concerns of a new arms race. China is identified as the fastest-growing nuclear power, while major nations are modernizing arsenals and increasing deployable warheads despite declining stockpiles.

title: 'In Pakistan, they used to stand with guns outside room': Litton on B'desh's T20 WC withdrawal
content: Bangladesh captain Litton Das has again denied that players decided to skip the 2026 T20 World Cup in India. He said the squad wanted to play and was only informed of the decision, not consulted. Challenging safety concerns, Litton noted Bangladesh had toured Pakistan despite tighter security, saying, “they used to stand with guns outside the room.”

title: 'Organised factory of hate': India tears into Pakistan's 'Fitna al Hindustan' campaign at UN
content: India has strongly condemned Pakistan's alleged state-sponsored propaganda campaign at the United Nations, accusing Islamabad of labeling militant groups as 'Fitna al Hindustan' to deflect blame. New Delhi highlighted Pakistan's internal challenges and military's influence, while also holding it responsible for civilian casualties in Afghanistan due to cross-border military operations.

title: 'ED targeting Hindu traders': Arvind Kejriwal attacks Centre over Punjab raids
content: Enforcement Directorate officials conducted raids at the Jalandhar residence and office of businessman Amit Bajaj, reportedly close to the Aam Aadmi Party.  In response, AAP leaders, including Arvind Kejriwal, framed the action as harassment of 'Hindu traders' in Punjab, urging them not to panic and pledging government support against the central agency.

title: 'She did what the director asked her to do': Jagapathi Babu backs Janhvi in 'Peddi' controversy
content: Amidst the controversy over Janhvi Kapoor's performance in 'Peddi', seasoned actor Jagapathi Babu has come to her defense. He highlighted that Kapoor acted in alignment with director Buchi Babu Sana's artistic intent. Babu encouraged detractors to shift their focus to the film's creative aspects instead of targeting Kapoor herself, noting that actors typically have little say in the final product.

title: Big relief for Khan Sir: Patna court stays arrest in coaching centre firing case
content: Educator Khan Sir received a reprieve as a Patna court stayed his arrest in a case involving an alleged firing at his coaching institute. The incident, linked to a rivalry, saw security guards claim they acted on his orders, leading to an FIR.

title: Supreme Court directs CBSE to release revaluation result by Friday
content: The CBSE has concluded its application window for Class 12 marks verification and re-evaluation, which ran from June 2 to June 7. Over 1.6 lakh students submitted requests for more than 3.8 lakh answer books, despite facing cyber threats. While the board assures a transparent process, some students reported delays in receiving scanned answer sheets

title: Pediatrician shares 3 parenting lessons Indian parents can learn from Swiss families
content: Swiss parenting offers valuable lessons for global parents. Dr. Ravi Malik observed children gaining independence early. Public scolding is avoided, replaced by respectful guidance. Outdoor play is prioritized for at least two hours daily. These practices build confidence, independence, and social skills. The focus is on understanding the values behind these methods.

title: Stokes’s England captaincy under serious threat after nightclub incident
content: Ben Stokes’s England captaincy is under threat after he and Gus Atkinson allegedly broke a team curfew and became involved in a nightclub altercation following the win over New Zealand. The ECB is investigating, though sources say the players “were not the aggressors.” A security guard was injured, Stokes may miss the next Test, and Harry Brook is expected to captain.

title: 28,000 vehicles an hour and growing; Cyberabad bets on  flyovers and underpasses to ease Allwyn-Miyapur traffic snarls
content: The road project includes a 1.79-km bi-directional bridge and flyover covering both the Miyapur and Allwyn junctions, along with two uni-directional vehicle underpasses at Miyapur Junction measuring 633 metres and 825 metres respectively

title: Modi government intoxicated on power: Congress on reduction in number of subsidised Ujjwala refills
content: The Congress president said the LPG prices have been hiked repeatedly and 5.56 crore beneficiaries are unable to afford refills

title: Coaching centre firing case: Patna court stays arrest of ‘Khan Sir’
content: The court also rejected the bail plea of Raushan Anand, the rival coaching institute owner and teacher who was earlier arrested in the case

title: Muslim Community representatives, civil societies to establish task force to tackle hate crimes
content: “The meeting is not a confrontation against any government or political party; rather, it is a sincere effort to find sustainable and practical solutions to the issues faced by Muslims,” said Maulana Ilyas Khan Falahi, president, Jamaat-e-Islami Maharashtra

title: Dressing up massacre as military operation does not absolve perpetrator: India on Pakistan at UNSC
content: Strongly condemning the airstrikes on Afghan territory, India said these are flagrant violations of international law, the UN Charter and the principle of state sovereignty

title: Lottery baron Santiago Martin, MLA wife Leema Rose and daughter Daisy move Madras High Court against ED action
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan order notice to the Central agency on batch of 39 appeals filed against the attachment of properties worth ₹910.29 crore

title: Ahmedabad plane crash: Families of AI 171 crash victims grapple with flight fears, anxiety
content: For many families and eyewitnesses, the tragedy did not end with the crash and they still live with a deep fear of air travel

title: 8 dead, 6 critical after explosion at Rashtriya Ispat Nigam Limited plant
content: The major industrial accident occurred at RINL, the corporate entity of the Visakhapatnam Steel Plant (VSP), when a ladle exploded and released hundreds of tonnes of molten steel; Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu expresses grief over the mishap

title: ED summons ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan’s daughter T. Veena for questioning in CMRL pay-off case
content: Besides Veena, ED asks CMRL chairman and managing director Sasidharan Kartha and senior company officials to appear before it as part of probe

title: Reddy dynasty’s gift to a Siva temple surfaces in a 14th-century Kondaveedu inscription
content: A broken 14th-century Telugu inscription at Kondaveedu Fort points to Reddy-era grants for worship at a hilltop Siva temple

title: Landmark feat: ‘Breakthrough’ in Zojila tunnel achieved
content: The breakthrough has brought the decades-old dream of all-weather connectivity between the Kashmir Valley and Ladakh closer to reality

title: Heavy rain likely in Rayalaseema, South Coastal A.P. on June 9, 10
content: Several districts are likely to record day temperatures between 42°C and 43°, says Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority

title: Former Maharashtra Minister files plea in Supreme Court against CBSE's three-language rule for Class 9 students
content: The plea filed by Fauzia Khan, an educationist and former Maharashtra Minister, contends that the CBSE's May 15 circular is arbitrary and unreasonable

title: Foreigners who returned after voting in T.N. Assembly election come under the scanner
content: Central and State law-enforcement agencies are working with the Election Commission of India to obtain data on foreign nationals who might have voted. Investigators have sought the assistance of the Bureau of Immigration in identifying foreign nationals who arrived in the State ahead of the election and departed within two to three days of polling

title: Indian diaspora groups welcome court ruling striking down $100,000 H-1B visa fee
content: A federal judge on Monday (June 8, 2026) struck down the $100,000 fee imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump for H-1B applications

title: Chief Minister travels by Metro to avoid Zero-Traffic arrangements in Bengaluru
content: Shivakumar is visiting Kanakapura, his home turf, first time after becoming the Karnataka Chief Minister to meet and extend his gratitude to the people of his constituency

title: Whirlwinds batter Kerala’s Thrissur, cause widespread damage and traffic disruptions
content: Houses damaged, trees uprooted across multiple places; orange alert issued as heavy rain continues in Thrissur; no casualties reported

title: Rebel TMC MPs siding with BJP, says Kalyan Banerjee
content: ‘Today their leader is Narendra Modi; they have become BJP,’ says Kalyan Banerjee, Chief Whip of AITC in the Lok Sabha, on the rebel Trinamool Congress MPs

title: Sanjeev Arora case: ED conducts fresh raids in Punjab, Uttar Pradesh
content: Sanjeev Arora was arrested by the Central agency last month following day-long searches at his official residence in Chandigarh

title: Five-day BRICS meeting begins in Indore to discuss global issues in agriculture sector
content: As part of this conference, the Agriculture Working Group will hold deliberations till June 11 with officials from member countries

title: Notorious thief ‘Kozhi Paulose’ arrested by Thrissur Rural police
content: 68-year-old is accused of thefts in nearly 100 places of worship across Thrissur and Ernakulam in Kerala

title: Monsoon enters South Konkan, State advises farmers to defer sowing
content: The Agriculture and Disaster Management Department has forecast rainfall in parts of Sindhudurg and Ratnagiri districts until June 9; in other regions of Maharashtra, the monsoon’s progress and rainfall intensity are expected to remain low over the next week.

title: Consensual pre-marital relationship is not a blot on character, says Supreme Court
content: The top court observes that premarital relationships are common in contemporary society and cannot be used as a pretext to cast aspersions on a person’s character

title: Kumaraswamy announces ₹25 lakh and a job for kin of Visakhapatnam steel plant blast victims
content: The Steel Minister visits RINL and the hospital, orders an independent inquiry, and announces jobs and aid for the families of the dead and the injured

title: Congress’ electoral strategy weakened TMC, CPI(M) and DMK: Thirumavalavan
content: Speaking at a meeting of the INDIA bloc in New Delhi, he says the party’s approach has caused dissatisfaction among several constituents and calls upon it to explore ways to further strengthen and consolidate the alliance

title: Government 3% share sale in NLC India opens for subscription
content: The government is selling about 4.16 crore shares or up to 3% stake in NLC India (Neyveli Lignite Corporation) at a floor price of ₹303/share

title: World Oceans Day 2026: How diving is changing lives in Andaman’s Karen and Ranchi communities
content: The ocean has always fed the Karen and Ranchi communities. Now, it is helping them build careers, concrete homes and futures in a way their ancestors never imagined

title: What is Zojila Tunnel? All you need to know about the all weather tunnel in Ladakh
content: The 13.14 km tunnel cuts through the Himalayas at an altitude of 11,578 feet and connects the Kashmir Valley with Kargil in Ladakh

title: No fee hike for medical courses this year in Karnataka
content: Of the medical seats available in private medical colleges, 40% are filled under government quota, 40% under private quota, 15% under NRI quota, and 5% under management quota

title: Robust evidence supported by data can strengthen public health policies: Karnataka health secretary
content: Around 300 persons are participating in a three-day conference on the theme of future-focused health systems

title: West Bengal joins Ayushman Bharat scheme
content: “The portability feature of PM-JAY would be particularly beneficial for migrant workers from West Bengal and their families. Beneficiaries working or residing in other States would be able to access cashless treatment at nearby empanelled hospitals across the countr,’’ said Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda

title: Karnataka High Court upholds law allowing BWSSB to lay sewer through private properties without acquisition of land
content: Pointing out that the provisions of the Act requires the BWSSB to cause as little damage as possible and mandates full compensation for damage caused, the court says that ‘the statutory scheme, therefore, does not contemplate unrestricted occupation or appropriation of land...’

title: Consumer data migration: BESCOM halts online services for 21 days in Bengaluru
content: BESCOM officials stated that essential online features, including application for new electricity connection, name transfer, load enhancement request, and online bill payments, will not be available from June 10 to 30

title: Irregular collection of garbage  leads to proliferation of blackspots in Bengaluru
content: The NammaKasa portal, which tracks blackspots in the city, reported 5,445 active blackspots on Sunday (June 7); just a month earlier, the number stood at 3,200

title: 85 confirmed cases of shigellosis reported till June in Kerala; preventive measures initiated
content: While the infection is self-limiting in healthy adults, it can be fatal for children under five years, in whom the highest mortality is reported. The elderly and immunocompromised individuals are also highly vulnerable

title: 24 Indian sailors rescued from ship hit by U.S. missile
content: Marivex sanctioned by the U.S. last December, had earlier carried Iranian oil to India; U.S. Central Command says the tanker violated the ongoing blockade by attempting to sail to an Iranian port; sailors likely to reach India in two days

title: Dalit teen beaten to death in Tehri Garhwal over inter-caste friendship
content: According to a complaint lodged by Ketan's father, Dhanpal Lal, the incident occurred on June 8 night in Kholgarh village

title: APSRTC employees’ unions warn of stir over electric bus ‘privatisation’ plans
content: The government and APSRTC are bent on handing over electric buses, but also the bus depots to private operators on lease, say JAC convenors

title: President Murmu to confer Padma awards on June 23
content: The President has approved the conferment of 131 Padma awards this year, including to actors Mammootty and R. Madhavan, as well as the late Satish Shah, whose kin will receive the honour posthumously

title: CM Omar Abdullah seeks INDIA bloc’s support for J&K Statehood restoration
content: Jammu and Kashmir was reduced to a Union Territory in 2019 and its special status scrapped. The Supreme Court had called for early restoration of Statehood after 2024 Assembly election

title: Sabarimala ‘gold theft’: SIT submits report in Kerala High Court
content: It pertains to the investigation into the gold-plated/gold-clad copper plates of the temple, which had been sent to the National Metallurgical Laboratory, Jamshedpur, in March

title: India issues fresh Iran travel warning, urges citizens to leave
content: In view of the latest developments in the region, the Embassy advises all Indian nationals to avoid travel to Iran and those currently in Iran to exit the country using available transport

title: Rajya Sabha polls: M.P. Congress mulls moving MLAs away as BJP fields candidate on third seat
content: The BJP’s move pushed the Congress into alert mode with Leader of Opposition in the Assembly Umang Singhar hosting party MLAs and senior leaders over dinner at his residence and to discuss the election strategy.

title: PDP leaders plough paddy fields to protest govt. takeover  of land from farmers in Pulwama
content: Shabir Ahmad, a farmer from Pulwama’s Padgampora, said small patches of 3-4 kanals of land were being utilised by locals to procure rice and feed for cattle; “If the government takes the land, it will have a severe impact on locals,” said Mr. Ahmad.

title: 28 lakh candidates for 32,679 vacancies: Oppn. attacks U.P. govt. over ‘mounting unemployment’
content: According to the Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board (UPPRPB), 28,86,797 candidates have applied to compete for 32,679 vacancies over three days (June 8, 9 and 10) across the State.

title: President Murmu confers 7 Kirti Chakras; IAF pilot, Gaganyatri Prasanth Nair among recipients
content: Lt. Commander Dilna K. and Lt. Commander Roopa A. of the Navy are among the Shaurya Chakra recipients after completion of Navika Sagar Parikrama II; Sepoy Janjal Pravin Prabhakar of the Mahar Regiment of Rashtriya Rifles and Lt. Shashank Tiwari of the Sikkim Scouts have been conferred the Kirti Chakra posthumously

title: SGPC gets visas for 541 Sikh pilgrims to travel to Pakistan on martyrdom anniversary of Guru Arjan Dev
content: During the visit, the pilgrims will pay obeisance at various historic Sikh shrines and participate in commemorative events before returning to India on June 19

title: INDIA bloc to take SIR complaints to CJI; seeks Pradhan’s immediate exit
content: It urges Centre to convene all-party meeting on current economic situation; parties question Congress for parting ways with DMK and for criticising then Pinarayi govt.

title: Toofan gains momentum as police intensify anti-drug operations in Ernakulam
content: Within Kochi city limits, police recorded 129 cases and arrested 145 accused under the NDPS Act in the week since the operation began on June 1. In Perumbavoor, 11 migrant labourers were arrested for drug peddling and consumption during a day-and-night raid on Sunday

title: Trinamool Split Brings NDA Close To Its 2/3rd Majority Goal In Lok Sabha
content: The NDA currently falls short of a two-thirds majority in the Lok Sabha - a deficit that prevented the passage of the Women's Reservation Constitution Amendment Bill in April. The Women's Bill had been tied up with the Delimitation Bill and that too

title: Bengal Politics LIVE Updates: Churn Within Trinamool After Poll Loss, MP Says Rebels "Power Hungry"
content: West Bengal Politics LIVE Updates: Crisis within the party escalated dramatically with a full-blown rebellion in the legislature and Parliament wings.

title: This Actor-MP Has Been In Trinamool Since 2009. She Lists Why They Rebelled
content: Satabdi Roy was chosen yesterday as the deputy leader of a group of Trinamool MPs who met with BJP leaders.

title: Airlines Spent 78% More On Jet Fuel Despite Lesser Utilisation: US Data
content: US carriers spent $6.5 billion on fuel in April, compared with about $3.6 billion a year earlier, according to the Bureau of Transportation Statistics

title: Apple Executive Says Some Firms Are Building AI Without Regard For Humans
content: Apple's senior vice president of software engineering Craig Federighi made the remark during his keynote speech at Apple WWDC 2026

title: Outdoor Hospitals, Cut-off Communities As Philippine Earthquake Kills 41
content: In the hardest-hit Sarangani province, some areas remain accessible only by helicopter, and fears of aftershocks were slowing rescue efforts, local officials told reporters at a Tuesday briefing.

title: US Army's 1st Apache Gunship Crashes Near Hormuz, Both Pilots Rescued
content: The US military has used Apaches, along with MQ-9 Reaper drones and F/A-18 and F-35 fighter jets, as part of Central Command's operation challenging Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz to most commercial traffic.

title: Modinomics At 12: Chips And Charters
content: The government's stated roadmap of four plants operational by end-2026 aspires to put India among the world's top five chip ecosystems by 2029.

title: Zepto's Rs 8,010 Crore IPO: Revenue, Losses Up, What Investors Must Know
content: Zepto IPO: The company's net loss narrowed to Rs 1,538.67 crore in the March quarter from Rs 1,831.91 crore a year earlier.

title: Not Bengaluru, Not IT: This City, Sector Lead India's Pay Hike Race
content: Engineering is expected to post average salary increments of 9.9% in FY27, making it the fastest-growing function. IT follows closely at 9.8%.

title: Pentagon Labels Alibaba, Baidu, BYD As Aiding Chinese Military
content: This year's list has grown to 188 Chinese entities, up from last year's roughly 130 named by the Pentagon.

title: US To Offer Expedited Visa Interviews At Select Embassies For $750
content: The move is a potential effort to ease conditions caused by the Trump administration's push to make entering the United States more difficult.

title: US Iran War Live: Crew Rescued After US Helicopter Goes Down Near Hormuz
content: US Iran War LIVE Updates: Iran closed the airspace around Tehran's Imam Khomeini International Airport, the country's main airfield, after the Israeli attack.

title: Killing The Mood: Smartphones Reduce Birth Rate, Say Studies
content: As governments around the world struggle with ways to reverse plunging birth rates, new US studies suggest they have ignored a...

title: "In Spirit Of Friendship": Israel To Install Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's Statue In A 'Big' City
content: Revach said that to make the memorial worthy of Shivaji's legacy, Israel has sought cooperation from Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis regarding historical references, artistic consultation, and design considerations, and "he immediately a

title: Telangana Villagers Pass Resolution To Send Children To Government Schools
content: Villagers, largely from the farming community, held detailed deliberations in the panchayat and passed a resolution that all children from the village should be enrolled in government schools.

title: Video: Helicopter Rescue For 24 Indians From Ship Hit By Missile Near Oman
content: Visuals showed Omani helicopters in action, flying over the vessel's deck and evacuating the Indian crew members from the Palau-flagged oil tanker.

title: United Nations Warns Of 'Deepening Crisis' In Oceans, Urges Action
content: Oceans are in a "deepening crisis" that demands urgent global action, a major UN report warned Monday, with seas warming and rising faster, ice cover shrinking...

title: Three reports, one story: MiraOne to usher in new era of precision medicine through single blood test; grand launch in Mumbai
content: a comprehensive blood test that promises to transform the way we study our body was launched on Saturday in Mumbai at ImagiNXT conference attended by an impressive audience . MiraOne generates three reports to tell one story from a single draw of blood . gives a complete picture - your blood markers, the DNA you inherited and how you respond to medicines .
